@@ -58,7 +58,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","title":"Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step","description":"# Task: Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step ## Context Part of EPIC: Pre-Flight Validation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0ok) ## Rationale Users who skip the wizard and run the installer directly should still get pre-flight validation. Currently they only see failures 15 minutes in. ## Proposal Add preflight as Phase 0 of install.sh: ```bash run_phase 0 "Pre-Flight Validation" run_preflight_checks ``` ### Behavior - Run all preflight checks - On FAIL: Show errors, exit (don't waste time) - On WARN: Show warnings, ask to continue (unless --yes) - On PASS: Continue silently ### Flag ```bash --skip-preflight # Skip pre-flight checks (for debugging) ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Preflight runs before Phase 1 - Failures abort immediately with actionable message - Warnings shown but don't block (with --yes) - --skip-preflight available for edge cases ## Files to Modify - install.sh: Add Phase 0, add flag - scripts/preflight.sh: Ensure can be sourced, not just executed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:50:00.154616-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated preflight into install.sh as Phase 0. Added run_preflight_checks() function, --skip-preflight flag, and auto-download for curl|bash scenario.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0iq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:28.766175-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-58by","title":"Refactor install_helpers.sh to remove duplicate feature-flag logic","description":"scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh currently contains duplicated/overlapping implementations for generated-vs-legacy category feature flags (multiple helpers and a later redefinition of acfs_use_generated_category). Consolidate to a single implementation, remove dead code, and add a small shell test to lock behavior.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:08:53.010083-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (generated category flag logic consolidated)"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","title":"Task: Create command reference page","description":"# Create Command Reference Page ## Parent Feature Reference Documentation (1y8) ## Goal Create a searchable command reference page at `/learn/commands` that lists all commands installed by ACFS. ## Design ### Page Layout ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Command Reference │ │ │ │ [🔍 Search commands...] │ │ │ │ Category: [All] [Agents] [Search] [Git] [System] │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ cc [Copy] │ │ │ │ Claude Code - AI coding assistant │ │ │ │ Example: cc "fix the bug in auth.ts" │ │ │ │ [Full docs →] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ cod [Copy] │ │ │ │ Codex CLI - OpenAI coding agent │ │ │ │ ... │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ... │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Command Data Structure ```typescript // lib/commands.ts interface Command { name: string; fullName: string; description: string; category: 'agents' | 'search' | 'git' | 'system' | 'stack'; example: string; docsUrl?: string; aliases?: string[]; } export const COMMANDS: Command[] = [ // Agents { name: 'cc', fullName: 'Claude Code', description: 'AI coding assistant', category: 'agents', example: 'cc "fix the bug"' }, { name: 'cod', fullName: 'Codex CLI', description: 'OpenAI coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'cod "add tests"' }, { name: 'gmi', fullName: 'Gemini CLI', description: 'Google coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'gmi "review code"' }, // Search { name: 'rg', fullName: 'ripgrep', description: 'Ultra-fast code search', category: 'search', example: 'rg "pattern" ./src' }, { name: 'fd', fullName: 'fd-find', description: 'Fast file finder', category: 'search', example: 'fd "*.ts"' }, { name: 'fzf', fullName: 'Fuzzy Finder', description: 'Interactive fuzzy search', category: 'search', example: 'fzf' }, // Git { name: 'lazygit', fullName: 'LazyGit', description: 'Visual git interface', category: 'git', example: 'lazygit' }, { name: 'git', fullName: 'Git', description: 'Version control', category: 'git', example: 'git status' }, // System { name: 'ntm', fullName: 'Named Tmux Manager', description: 'Session management', category: 'system', example: 'ntm new project' }, { name: 'lsd', fullName: 'LSDeluxe', description: 'Modern ls replacement', category: 'system', example: 'lsd -la' }, { name: 'bat', fullName: 'Bat', description: 'Cat with syntax highlighting', category: 'system', example: 'bat file.ts' }, { name: 'zoxide', fullName: 'Zoxide', description: 'Smart cd replacement', category: 'system', example: 'z project' }, // Stack { name: 'bd', fullName: 'Beads CLI', description: 'Task graph management', category: 'stack', example: 'bd list' }, { name: 'bv', fullName: 'Beads Viewer', description: 'Kanban task viewer', category: 'stack', example: 'bv' }, { name: 'ubs', fullName: 'Ultimate Bug Scanner', description: 'Code quality scanner', category: 'stack', example: 'ubs .' }, { name: 'cass', fullName: 'CASS', description: 'Session search', category: 'stack', example: 'cass' }, { name: 'cm', fullName: 'CASS Memory', description: 'Procedural memory', category: 'stack', example: 'cm context "auth"' }, { name: 'caam', fullName: 'CAAM', description: 'Account manager', category: 'stack', example: 'caam status' }, { name: 'am', fullName: 'Agent Mail', description: 'Agent coordination', category: 'stack', example: 'am status' }, ]; ``` ### Features 1. **Search** - Filter by command name or description 2. **Category tabs** - Quick filter by category 3. **Copy button** - One-click copy command 4. **Expandable** - Show full usage on click 5. **Deep links** - `/learn/commands#cc` jumps to command ### SEO - Title: "ACFS Command Reference" - Description: "Quick reference for all commands installed by Agent Flywheel" - Keywords: individual command names ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Page accessible at `/learn/commands` - [ ] All ACFS commands listed - [ ] Search filters results in real-time - [ ] Category tabs work - [ ] Copy button copies command - [ ] Mobile-friendly layout ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/lib/commands.ts` - Create: `apps/web/app/learn/commands/page.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/command-ref-card.tsx`","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:22:20.811137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented /learn/commands with search, category filters, deep links, and copyable examples.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-1y8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:10.574895-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","title":"Add SSH key verification step (check files exist)","description":"# Task: Add SSH Key Verification Step ## Parent Epic [EPIC] SSH Key Generation Clarity ## Problem After running ssh-keygen, users don't know how to verify it worked or understand what was created. They have two files (public/private) but don't know which is which or what they're for. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Add verification section after the command: \`\`\`tsx

You should see two files:

  • acfs_ed25519 — Your private key (keep this secret!)
  • acfs_ed25519.pub — Your public key (this gets shared)
 Think of it like a mailbox: the public key is your address (you share it), the private key is your mailbox key (only you have it). \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows verification command - [ ] Explains the two files and their purposes - [ ] Uses relatable analogy (mailbox) - [ ] Reassures user about what to keep secret ## Why This Matters Users who don't verify their key might not realize when something went wrong. The public/private distinction prevents confusion later when they need to share their public key.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:25:36.905528-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-unqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:12.253977-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","title":"Add SSH key verification step (check files exist)","description":"# Task: Add SSH Key Verification Step ## Parent Epic [EPIC] SSH Key Generation Clarity ## Problem After running ssh-keygen, users don't know how to verify it worked or understand what was created. They have two files (public/private) but don't know which is which or what they're for. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Add verification section after the command: \`\`\`tsx

You should see two files:

  • acfs_ed25519 — Your private key (keep this secret!)
  • acfs_ed25519.pub — Your public key (this gets shared)
 Think of it like a mailbox: the public key is your address (you share it), the private key is your mailbox key (only you have it). \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows verification command - [ ] Explains the two files and their purposes - [ ] Uses relatable analogy (mailbox) - [ ] Reassures user about what to keep secret ## Why This Matters Users who don't verify their key might not realize when something went wrong. The public/private distinction prevents confusion later when they need to share their public key.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:21.115008-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:21.115008-05:00","close_reason":"Added 'Verify Your Key Was Created' section with ls command to check both key files exist, explanation of private vs public key, and mailbox analogy","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-unqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:12.253977-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","title":"EPIC: VPS Installer (install.sh)","description":"# Epic: VPS Installer (install.sh) ## Overview Build the one-liner bash installer that transforms a fresh Ubuntu VPS into a fully-armed agentic coding environment. ## Background & Reasoning This is the "magic moment" - the user pastes one command and everything happens. The installer must: - Work when run as root (common VPS default) - Work when run as ubuntu with sudo - Be idempotent (safe to run twice) - Handle connection drops and resume - Log everything for debugging ## Design Philosophy **Vibe Mode Default**: Passwordless sudo, agents with full permissions, minimal friction. **Checkpointed Phases**: Each phase writes state so reruns skip completed work. **Module Manifest**: Single source of truth (acfs.manifest.yaml) drives all installs. ## Installer Phases 1. Preflight + confirm OS (Ubuntu 24.04+ / 25.x) 2. Create/normalize `ubuntu` user + sudoers 3. Base packages (apt) 4. Shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh + p10k + plugins) 5. Dev toolchain (bun, uv, rust, go, tmux, rg, ast-grep) 6. Agent stack (claude/codex/gemini CLIs + aliases) 7. Flywheel stack (ntm, slb, ubs, cass, cm, bv, caam, mcp_agent_mail) 8. Post-install UX (onboard + doctor + summary) ## Success Criteria - [ ] Fresh Ubuntu 25.x VPS → working environment in < 15 minutes - [ ] Safe to run twice without breaking anything - [ ] All 8 Dicklesworthstone tools installed and verified - [ ] `acfs doctor` shows all green - [ ] Logs available at /var/log/acfs/install.log ## Considerations - Always prefer upstream installers when available - Verify each tool after install - Provide clear error messages with fix suggestions - Support `--dry-run` for transparency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","close_reason":"All 12 sub-tasks completed. VPS installer fully modularized with libraries for: logging, OS detection, user normalization, shell setup, CLI tools, language runtimes, coding agents, Dicklesworthstone stack, cloud/database tools, smoke test, and canonical zshrc.","labels":["epic","installer"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","title":"Create installer logging library (scripts/lib/logging.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Installer Logging Library ## Description Build a reusable logging library for consistent installer output. ## Functions ```bash log_step() # [1/8] Step description (blue) log_detail() # Indented detail (gray) log_success() # ✔ Success message (green) log_warn() # ⚠️ Warning message (yellow) log_error() # ✖ Error message (red) log_debug() # Only if ACFS_DEBUG=1 ``` ## Requirements - All output to stderr (keep stdout clean) - Support --quiet flag (suppress non-errors) - Support --verbose flag (show debug) - Timestamps in log file - ANSI color codes with fallback ## Log File Write all output to: - `/var/log/acfs/install.log` - `~/.acfs/logs/install..log` ## Implementation ```bash #!/bin/bash # scripts/lib/logging.sh ACFS_LOG_FILE="/var/log/acfs/install.log" ACFS_QUIET="${ACFS_QUIET:-0}" ACFS_VERBOSE="${ACFS_VERBOSE:-0}" log_step() { local step="$1" local msg="$2" echo -e "\033[34m[$step] $msg\033[0m" >&2 echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] STEP: $msg" >> "$ACFS_LOG_FILE" } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All log functions implemented - [ ] Colors work in terminal - [ ] --quiet suppresses output - [ ] --verbose shows debug - [ ] Log file written with timestamps - [ ] Works when sourced by other scripts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.871116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.872789-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.10","title":"Create modern CLI tools installer (scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Modern CLI Tools Installer ## Description Install modern CLI replacements that the zshrc depends on. ## Tools to Install ### Via apt (Ubuntu 24.04+) - ripgrep (rg) - fast grep - fd-find (fd) - fast find - bat/batcat - better cat - fzf - fuzzy finder - tmux - terminal multiplexer - neovim - better vim ### Via cargo (more recent versions) - lsd - modern ls with icons - dust - better du - btop - better top (or via apt) - zoxide - better cd - ast-grep (sg) - structural grep ### Via curl/installer - lazygit - git TUI - lazydocker - docker TUI - direnv - directory-specific env ## Functions ```bash install_apt_cli_tools() install_cargo_cli_tools() install_other_cli_tools() verify_cli_tools() ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All tools installed - [ ] Fallback logic (apt first, cargo if newer needed) - [ ] Verification for each tool - [ ] Idempotent","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.469899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:27:23.299944-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:27:23.299944-05:00","close_reason":"Created scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh with modular installer functions for apt, cargo, and curl-based CLI tools","labels":["installer","mvp","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.10","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.472277-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -132,7 +132,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-db0","title":"Implement shell tool updates (omz, p10k, plugins, atuin, zoxide)","description":"## What Implement the 'shell' category for acfs-update: - Oh-My-Zsh: Run $ZSH/tools/upgrade.sh (non-interactive) - Powerlevel10k: git pull --ff-only in $ZSH_CUSTOM/themes/powerlevel10k - zsh-autosuggestions: git pull --ff-only in plugin dir - zsh-syntax-highlighting: git pull --ff-only in plugin dir - Atuin: Use atuin self-update if available, else reinstall - Zoxide: Reinstall via official installer ## Git-Based Updates (omz, p10k, plugins) - Use git pull --ff-only to avoid merge conflicts - If fast-forward fails: warn and skip (user has local changes) - OMZ has its own upgrade script that handles prompts ## Installer-Based Updates (Atuin, Zoxide) These use curl|sh installers like the stack tools: 1. Atuin: Try `atuin self-update` first (if available) - If no self-update: fetch installer, verify checksum, run 2. Zoxide: Fetch installer, verify checksum against checksums.yaml, run For checksum mismatches: same flow as stack tools (warn, prompt, skip/proceed) ## Considerations - ZSH_CUSTOM defaults to $ZSH/custom if not set - DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT=true for OMZ non-interactive - Some users may have customized themes/plugins - respect that ## Success Criteria - [ ] All shell tools update without prompts (in --yes mode) - [ ] Git-based updates handle conflicts gracefully - [ ] Atuin/Zoxide checksum verification works - [ ] Before/after versions logged - [ ] Skips missing components","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:10.463809-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:18:10.575757-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:18:10.575757-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented shell tool updates: omz, p10k, zsh plugins, atuin, zoxide","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-db0","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.018434-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dcy","title":"[codex.8] Audit web app for incorrect codex auth guidance","description":"## Goal Check apps/web/ for any incorrect guidance about codex-cli authentication. ## Files to Check Based on grep results: - apps/web/lib/jargon.ts - apps/web/app/wizard/run-installer/page.tsx - apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts - apps/web/app/workflow/page.tsx - apps/web/app/page.tsx ## What to Look For 1. Any mention of OPENAI_API_KEY for codex 2. Incorrect auth flow descriptions 3. Wizard steps that suggest API key setup ## Updates Needed 1. Replace API key guidance with OAuth flow description 2. Update any wizard steps about codex setup 3. Ensure jargon/definitions are correct ## Lower Priority Because The web app is less critical than the installer scripts - users who hit the web app will eventually run the installer which (after fixes) will give correct guidance. ## Acceptance Criteria 1. No incorrect OPENAI_API_KEY references for codex in web app 2. Correct OAuth flow described if mentioned","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:24.840054-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:56.25612-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:56.25612-05:00","close_reason":"Audited web app - no incorrect OPENAI_API_KEY references for codex found. Jargon entry describes Codex without auth details. CAAM references are about credential management (correct).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dcy","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:36.94908-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dfc","title":"Fix wizard layout heading causing mobile E2E failures","description":"Playwright mobile projects fail because WizardLayout renders a hidden (md:block)

step title before page content. Tests use page.locator('h1').first(), which resolves to the hidden layout heading on mobile. Also results in multiple h1s per page. Fix by removing or demoting layout heading, ensuring a single visible h1 per wizard page, and update tests if needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:03:08.330927-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:30:35.67678-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T21:30:35.67678-05:00","close_reason":"Already fixed on main: apps/web/app/wizard/layout.tsx no longer renders a hidden

step title; only the step indicator remains, so mobile h1 selector mismatch is resolved.","labels":["a11y","ci","e2e","web"]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dmff","title":"Add headless browser auth flow explanation","description":"# Task: Add Headless Browser Auth Flow Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj) ## Description When users run `claude` or `gh auth login` on the VPS, these tools try to open a browser. But there's no browser on the VPS! The tool prints a URL that must be opened on the user's laptop. This "device flow" is completely unexplained. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx Add explanation before the auth commands: \`\`\`tsx

Your VPS doesn't have a web browser, so authentication works a bit differently:

  1. Run the login command (like claude)
  2. The terminal will display a URL (or code)
  3. Copy that URL and paste it in your laptop's browser
  4. Complete the login in your browser
  5. Return to your terminal — it should confirm success
If you see "Opening browser..." but nothing happens, that's normal! Just copy the URL shown and open it manually.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains the device flow clearly - [ ] Step-by-step numbered list - [ ] Addresses "nothing happened" confusion - [ ] Appears before the auth command list - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Use Laptop icon to reinforce "use your laptop's browser" - This should feel like a helpful heads-up, not a warning - Consider adding a visual diagram showing the flow","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:42:00.199364-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:25:09.558506-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dmff","title":"Add headless browser auth flow explanation","description":"# Task: Add Headless Browser Auth Flow Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj) ## Description When users run `claude` or `gh auth login` on the VPS, these tools try to open a browser. But there's no browser on the VPS! The tool prints a URL that must be opened on the user's laptop. This "device flow" is completely unexplained. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx Add explanation before the auth commands: \`\`\`tsx

Your VPS doesn't have a web browser, so authentication works a bit differently:

  1. Run the login command (like claude)
  2. The terminal will display a URL (or code)
  3. Copy that URL and paste it in your laptop's browser
  4. Complete the login in your browser
  5. Return to your terminal — it should confirm success
If you see "Opening browser..." but nothing happens, that's normal! Just copy the URL shown and open it manually.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains the device flow clearly - [ ] Step-by-step numbered list - [ ] Addresses "nothing happened" confusion - [ ] Appears before the auth command list - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Use Laptop icon to reinforce "use your laptop's browser" - This should feel like a helpful heads-up, not a warning - Consider adding a visual diagram showing the flow","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:42:00.199364-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:19.796699-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive unit coverage (no mocks) + E2E logging","description":"# Goal Establish real-data, no-mock unit coverage and complete E2E integration scripts with detailed, human-readable logging. # Why - Current tests are partial (installer E2E + web Playwright). No full unit coverage across core Bash libs or manifest tooling. - E2E logs exist but are not standardized or granular enough for fast diagnosis. # Success Criteria - Unit coverage exists for manifest parser/generator, selection resolver, contract/security/preflight libs with real fixtures (no mocks/fakes). - E2E scripts cover bootstrap + selection + resume flows with structured logs and artifacts. - Tests are deterministic, diffable, and runnable locally + in CI. # Notes - "No mocks/fakes" means: use real manifests, real generated scripts, real flags; avoid stubbing core behaviors. - Logging should be timestamped, sectioned, and include command echo + exit codes. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:13.75871-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:16:59.6662-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:16:59.6662-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 children completed: coverage matrix, parser/generator tests (132 tests), installer lib tests (15 tests), test harness, selection E2E (26 tests), web E2E runner script.","labels":["qa","tests"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.1","title":"Define coverage matrix + real fixtures catalog","description":"# Scope Create a concrete coverage matrix that maps each core component to a test type, and standardize a catalog of **real** fixtures used by unit tests. # Deliverables - Coverage matrix table (component → test type → owner → status). - Fixture catalog list (real manifest YAMLs, generated scripts, seed state files) with storage location and usage notes. # Detailed comments - Components to include: `packages/manifest` (schema/parser/generator), `scripts/lib` (install_helpers, contract, security, preflight, logging), `install.sh` selection/introspection, `tests/vm` E2E. - Fixtures must be real (valid YAMLs and generated outputs), not synthetic mocks. - Ensure fixtures are minimal but realistic; avoid brittle snapshots. # Acceptance criteria - Matrix is stored in repo (likely `docs/tests/coverage_matrix.md`). - Fixture catalog references actual files committed in repo.
